title: Publish machine-readable contracts for complex tools
goal: Move the normative precondition, state-transition, idempotency, retry and terminal-outcome rules of the most complex Grabowski surfaces into versioned machine-readable contracts and make prose subordinate to those contracts.

acceptance.assertion: Versioned contracts cover at least Agent Workspace lifecycle, durable task/job lifecycle and irreversible or secret-backed operation families.
Each contract defines states, allowed transitions, required evidence, idempotency key or identity, retry eligibility, stable failure codes, terminal outcomes and authority boundaries.
Tests or generators prove that implementation enums, response fields and documentation do not drift from the contract.
Existing receipts remain readable with explicit legacy classification and cannot acquire new retry or success authority merely because a new contract exists.
Long-form documentation is generated from the contracts or checked against generated state and retry tables; prose is explanatory, not the sole normative source.
Consumer-facing contract views expose only the relevant state table and stable fields without returning the full internal schema by default.
Tests reject unknown transitions, missing evidence, stale identities, contradictory terminal outcomes and prose or enum drift.
